name: Service Creation
description: The registration of a new service or daemon on an operating system. Adversaries often create services to establish persistence, ensuring their malicious code runs automatically at system startup or in the background with elevated privileges.

event_logs.description: Captures the creation of a new Windows service.
Captures services installed by administrators or adversaries.
Service crash or unexpected stop; could indicate malicious modification or exploitation.

sysmon.description: Service state changes; detects service installation events.
Registry modifications; captures service persistence changes (e.g., modifying HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services).

powershell.description: Script Block Logging; monitor for specific service manipulation cmdlets.

audit_framework.description: Detects changes to systemd service configurations.

systemd.description: Captures logs for newly created systemd services.

launch_services.description: Monitor for new plist files indicating service creation.

log_sources.Name: auditd:CONFIG_CHANGE
log_sources.Channel: creation or modification of systemd services

log_sources.Name: containerLogs:systemd_unit_files
log_sources.Channel: unit file referencing container binary with persistent flags

log_sources.Name: linux:osquery
log_sources.Channel: newly registered unit file with ExecStart pointing to unknown binary

log_sources.Name: linux:syslog
log_sources.Channel: systemctl start/enable with uncommon binary paths

log_sources.Name: macos:osquery
log_sources.Channel: detection of new launch agents with suspicious paths or unsigned binaries

log_sources.Name: macos:unifiedlog
log_sources.Channel: creation or loading of new launchd services

log_sources.Name: macos:unifiedlog
log_sources.Channel: launchd loading new LaunchDaemon or changes to existing daemon configuration
